title: GraphQL create fallback — deploy GPUs the REST enum rejects (RTX PRO 4500 etc.)
description:
GOAL (user): the user can connect to ANY card the picker shows. Spun out of MPI-135; full brainstorm + 4-agent investigation + LIVE VERIFICATION done 2026-06-29.

=== ROOT CAUSE (live-confirmed) ===
The GPU picker lists cards from RunPod's GraphQL `gpuTypes` catalogue, but Pods CREATE via REST `POST /pods` whose `gpuTypeIds` is a SEPARATE static ENUM that lags the catalogue. 'NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell' shows pickable + available:true + stock High, but REST createPod 400s `At /pods/properties/gpuTypeIds/items/enum`. Live-probed: NO id variant ('NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell' / 'RTX PRO 4500' / 'NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500') passes the REST enum; controls 'NVIDIA RTX A4500' + 'NVIDIA RTX 4000 Ada Generation' DO pass (got 500 stock, not enum-reject). NOT a wrong-id bug — the card is genuinely absent from the REST enum. ('RTX PRO 4000 Blackwell' may not be offered by RunPod at all — verify it exists before relying on it.)

=== THE FIX (verified viable) — GraphQL create fallback, NOT grey-out ===
LIVE-PROVEN 2026-06-29: a raw GraphQL `podFindAndDeployOnDemand` for RTX PRO 4500 RETURNED A RUNNING POD (id oo8el8ng4rngcb, desiredStatus RUNNING, our cu128 image) — the exact card REST rejects. The pod was then DELETED + getPod-confirmed 404 via the REST routes — so REST manages a GraphQL-created pod fine (shared id namespace). GraphQL `gpuTypeId` is a FREE STRING (no enum), takes the same id the picker already has; the RunPod console uses this same mutation (why it works there). Mutation is ACTIVE, no deprecation/sunset (RunPod's own runpodctl + runpod-python still use it); REST is the 'new public face' but GraphQL is maintained — low risk for tactical use.

WHY NOT the original grey-out: grey-out HIDES these (and future) cards forever. The fallback ENABLES them and is a PERMANENT, ZERO-MAINTENANCE net — the app has NO hardcoded GPU values (picker = live GraphQL catalogue; podImageForCard branches only on a 'blackwell' name substring → cu128 else cu124, unknown→cu124). So any card RunPod adds to the catalogue but not the REST enum (now OR future) becomes createable with no code edits; if RunPod later adds it to REST, the primary path just works too. Self-maintaining.

=== DESIGN — hybrid, triggered by the gpuUnsupported flag MPI-135 ALREADY ships ===
1. REST create stays PRIMARY (keeps CPU download-mode pods + ALL the MPI-135 error handling intact). getPod / status / specs / stats / stop / delete / reconnect ALL stay REST, untouched.
2. When REST create enum-rejects → the existing `_createPodInternal` already detects it and returns `gpuUnsupported:true`. THAT is the fallback trigger: instead of returning the failure, call a new GraphQL create with the same params translated.
3. Add `client.createPodGraphql(key, spec)` (or similar) in routes/runpodRemote.js using `podFindAndDeployOnDemand` (mutation { podFindAndDeployOnDemand(input:{...}){ id desiredStatus imageName machineId } }). Adapt the GraphQL return shape ({data:{...}} | {errors:[...]}, always HTTP 200, no {ok,status,json} wrapper) to whatever _createPodInternal expects (extract .data.podFindAndDeployOnDemand.id).
4. PARAM TRANSLATION (REST spec → GraphQL input): gpuTypeIds:[id] → gpuTypeId:id (single string); dataCenterIds:[dc] → dataCenterId:dc; env:{K:V} object → env:[{key,value}] array; ports:['8889/http'] array → ports:'8889/http' comma-string; cloudType:SECURE; keep imageName, containerDiskInGb, networkVolumeId, volumeMountPath, volumeInGb, gpuCount. Confirm minVcpuCount/minMemoryInGb defaults (the probe sent 1/1).
5. CPU pods (computeType:CPU + cpuFlavorIds, MPI-88 download mode) MUST stay REST — GraphQL create has no documented computeType/cpuFlavorIds. Never fall back for the __cpu__ sentinel.
6. ERROR HANDLING: GraphQL errors live in {errors:[{message}]} (HTTP 200), not a 4xx body — the MPI-135 gpuTypeIds/items/enum classifier is REST-shaped; the GraphQL path needs its own error read. A GraphQL create failure should surface honestly (real out-of-stock = retryable; a true bad-card = unsupported).
7. AUTO-RETRY interplay: today the shell breaks the retry loop on gpuUnsupported (MPI-135). With the fallback, gpuUnsupported should instead route to the GraphQL create. If the GraphQL create ALSO fails for stock, THAT becomes the retryable signal (and DC-steering from MPI-135 A could apply). Re-check the shell `_runRemoteBoot` loop + the Settings `_connectEngine` handler so the new path doesn't dead-end or double-create.

=== PARITY GAPS (REST has, GraphQL create lacks) — only #1 matters for us ===
1. CPU pods — REST-only (handled: never fall back for __cpu__). 2. multi-GPU-type priority ordering, multi-DC ordering, countryCodes — we don't use these. So functional parity is FINE for GPU pods with volume + DC + env + ports + disk + count (all confirmed in GraphQL input).

=== BLAST RADIUS (from codebase agent) ===
Client split today: GraphQL = gpuTypes/dataCenters/availability (read only); REST = create/start/stop/delete/get/list pod + volumes/templates. createPod has ONE direct call site (_createPodInternal, used by /remote/pod/create AND the reconnect recreate-fallback). _graphql returns raw JSON (no ok/status); _rest returns {status,ok,json} — the adapter must bridge these. Switching ONLY create to GraphQL-on-fallback leaves _podRuntimeStatus (desiredStatus + machine.maintenanceStart/End/Note) and specs/stats UNAFFECTED (all getPod = REST). No prior GraphQL→REST migration exists to re-break (REST was picked at MPI-64 simply because it's RunPod's documented Pod-CRUD API; GraphQL was catalogue-only from day one).

=== VERIFY (acceptance) ===
(1) Connect RTX PRO 4500 in EU-RO-1 → REST enum-rejects → app silently falls back to GraphQL → pod boots → connects + generates, end-to-end. (2) A normal card (e.g. 5090) still creates via REST primary (no fallback fired). (3) CPU download-mode pod still creates via REST (no fallback). (4) A GraphQL-created pod stops/reconnects/deletes via the existing REST lifecycle. (5) maintenance bad-host detect (MPI-135 C) still fires on a GraphQL-created pod (getPod stays REST). (6) Out-of-stock on the GraphQL path surfaces honestly / retries, doesn't dead-end.

=== REFERENCES ===
GraphQL mutation shape + parity table: this card's events.jsonl (investigation 2026-06-29) + docs/gotchas.md § GraphQL↔REST GPU-id desync. Live probe method: a temporary POST /runpod/_graphql-probe raw passthrough (added + REMOVED this session — re-add if needed to re-probe). Mutation docs: docs.runpod.io/sdks/graphql/manage-pods, graphql-spec.runpod.io.
